recipes.title: Sourdough Focaccia
recipes.description: Thick, pillowy focaccia with a crisp base and olive-oil dimpled top. Great with anything or on its own.
ingredients: Flaky sea salt and rosemary to finish
steps: Mix flour and water, rest 30 min (autolyse).
Add starter and salt, fold until combined.
Perform 3 sets of stretch-and-folds over 3 hours.
Coat a baking tray generously with olive oil, turn dough in.
Cold-proof in the fridge overnight (8–16 h).
Pull from fridge 2 h before baking. Dimple with fingers, drizzle oil.
Bake at 220 °C for 25 min until deep golden.
Finish with flaky salt and rosemary straight from the oven.

recipes.title: Miso Butter Pasta
recipes.description: Ten-minute weeknight pasta. White miso and butter make an umami sauce that coats every strand.
ingredients: Parmesan and black pepper to serve
steps: Cook pasta in well-salted water. Reserve 100 ml pasta water before draining.
In a pan over medium heat, melt butter and gently fry garlic 1 min.
Off the heat, whisk miso into butter until smooth.
Add pasta and 80 ml pasta water, toss vigorously over low heat until sauce coats.
Serve immediately with Parmesan and cracked pepper.

recipes.title: Brown Butter Financiers
recipes.description: Small French almond cakes with crispy edges and a tender, nutty crumb. Perfect with coffee.
steps: Brown butter in a saucepan over medium heat until golden and nutty. Cool slightly.
Whisk together icing sugar, almond flour, flour, and salt.
Stir in egg whites and vanilla until just combined.
Stream in warm brown butter while stirring.
Rest batter 30 min in the fridge.
Fill greased financier moulds (or mini muffin tin) ¾ full.
Bake at 200 °C for 12–14 min until edges are deep golden and centres just set.
Cool 5 min, then unmould.
